architecture_summary.decision_boundary: Lean checks formal proofs; people review public meaning; release software preserves only relationships that people explicitly recorded.
five_parts: release checks and GitHub continuous integration
architecture_summary.purpose: This public repository separates five parts: Lean source, a human-reviewed record of selected public claims, authored reader documents, generated navigation views, and a release workflow that checks the relationships people explicitly recorded.

edges.relation: reconstructs deliberate edit types under an explicit evidence limit
projects release identity, open propositions, and mathematical non-claims
projects owners, routes, and validation commands
projects evaluation results and evidence residuals

nodes.role: claim status, release identity, open propositions, and source coordinates
shipped artifact inventory, content identity, entry routes, and evidence boundary
historical deliberately-altered-copy protocol, outcomes, limitations, and post-repair scope
separately versioned deterministic reconstruction, not original raw logs
generated bounded agent-entry projection, not proof or evidence authority

five_sentence_summary: Lean checks formal statements and proofs; it does not read the README or papers that explain them.
A mathematician reviews what selected formal results mean, how the public pages may describe them, and what remains open.

The reviewed result record is docs/claims.json, while docs/publication_contract.json separately inventories the papers and docs/publication_evidence.json separately retains historical checker evidence.
Generated views reorganise those records, and the release program plus GitHub workflow stop publication when a written relationship fails.
A passing release means the recorded comparisons succeeded; it does not mean software understood every public statement or proved that the list is complete.

historical_checker_example.plain_result: One false README edit passed because its public claim had not been recorded. After the claim was recorded, the intact text passed and the same false edit failed.
historical_checker_example.purpose: Illustrate the boundary of explicitly recorded checks; this is not an architecture component or reliability score.

historical_evidence_residuals.reentry_condition: Register authenticated original logs and bind their content identities before changing this boundary.
historical_evidence_residuals.statement: The original mutation run logs are not registered; historical timings remain a structured transcription.

historical_evidence_residuals.reentry_condition: Recover and authenticate an original target before marking another operator exact.
historical_evidence_residuals.statement: Only the explicitly named mutation operators preserve exact original targets; the remaining targets are deterministic reconstructions.

historical_evidence_residuals.reentry_condition: Keep later deterministic reconstructions separate from the historical result unless the original experiment is rerun with equivalent targets and archived logs.
historical_evidence_residuals.statement: The historical study did not rerun the other nine deliberately altered copies after the false README edit was repaired.

known_limitations: The mutation author and checker author were the same person.
Five of seven invariant families were exercised by exactly one mutation.
The study covers one corpus, one maintainer, and one working style.
There was no controlled comparison with ordinary continuous integration or disciplined manual review.
Reader error and transfer to other corpora were not measured.

explicitly_not_claimed: The release workflow is mathematical proof authority.
Every repository file is governed by one universal owner.
public_claims.statement: The repository names separate owners for formal proof, reviewed public meaning, paper identity, generated navigation, historical evidence, and release checks.

explicitly_not_claimed: The historical outcomes are a reliability score or future detection probability.
This is a controlled comparison with ordinary continuous integration.
The author of the deliberately altered copies was independent of the checker author.
public_claims.statement: The retained historical exercise records that several deliberately altered copies were rejected by the release program.

explicitly_not_claimed: The release program can infer every important public claim automatically.
public_claims.statement: One false README edit passed because the public claim it changed had not yet been listed among the relationships the release program must preserve.

explicitly_not_claimed: The original set of deliberate edits was rerun in full after the repair.
A post-repair ten-of-ten historical result exists.
public_claims.statement: After the public boundary was recorded, the intact text passed and the same false edit was rejected.

publication_non_claims: The release program does not understand arbitrary prose.
The selected comparisons and result labels are not proved complete.
There is no controlled comparison with ordinary continuous integration or disciplined manual review.
Byte budgets are not claimed to cause comprehension.
Transfer beyond this repository has not been demonstrated.
Porting and maintenance cost beyond recorded implementation size and runtime has not been measured.
Lean acceptance alone does not prove that a formal statement matches the intended informal mathematics.

registered_mathematical_non_claims.meaning: This release does not settle the universal Erdős #257 problem
This release does not settle Erdős #249
This release does not itself constitute a peer-reviewed publication record
This release is not asserted to be equivalent to any private development
Provider or model output is not proof authority; only the Lean kernel check is
No unpublished proof sketch or hidden artefact carries proof authority for this release

remaining_open_propositions.statement: Prove that S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ is irrational, with no bound on a possible rational denominator.
Produce certified non-integrality witnesses at unbounded parameters. A sufficient one-parameter form is to produce them along the lcm diagonal.
Decide whether 1/2 lies in the Mersenne achievement set. Yes refutes universal #257; no closes only this route.
Prove irrationality for every infinite support A, not only the named support families formalised here.

revision_and_identity.revision_embedding_boundary: A tracked file cannot contain its own Git commit id without self-reference. Content freshness is enforced by the owner hashes below; agents query the checkout revision at runtime.

thesis: This public repository separates five parts: Lean source, a human-reviewed record of selected public claims, authored reader documents, generated navigation views, and a release workflow that checks the relationships people explicitly recorded.
